what_it_does: Defines a propose-only flow that surfaces a 'dragnet_bake' inbox item for an operator, carrying the exact CLI and registry roles for a long-running extraction bake, without running the bake itself.
observations: The flow is marked 'proposes_only': true and never executes the side-effecting bake.
It builds and surfaces the exact operator command via _proposed_command using shlex.quote.
Payload includes optional design stage and the registry roles the bake will read.
run() imports store, fabric config, and governance Inbox inside the function body.
Parameters control grain asset, project filter, date range, and design stage.
standouts: proposes_only floor with explicit operator-only --confirm CLI door.
Exact, non-fabricated command string surfaced as part of the proposal.
References protected registry roles and schema-freeze/neutral-pass constraints in metadata and payload.
conventions: Heavy module docstring explaining governance rationale and constraints.
FLOW metadata dictionary describing id, label, params, and proposes_only flag.
Lazy function-local imports to avoid top-level coupling.
shlex quoting for safe command serialization.
concerns: run() does no validation or sanitization of out_name, projects, since, or until before building the command and payload.
Imports are nested inside run(), making dependencies and testability less obvious.
No error handling around Inbox.surface or FsStore construction.
ROOT path computation assumes a fixed directory nesting and may break if the file is moved or executed unusually.
notes: A governance shim that lets agents propose a heavy dragnet bake through the flows surface while keeping the actual execution behind an operator-only --confirm door.
questions: Where exactly is ops/dragnet_extract.py relative to this flow file, and is the command path correct in all deployment layouts?
What do the D1 schema-freeze and D3 neutral-coarse-pass constraints enforce in practice?
How does Inbox.surface behave when default='reject' and what does it return on rejection?
What is the content and interface of roles/dragnet_coarse.py, dragnet_fine.py, and dragnet_design.py?
What does the recommended '--sample N' sizing step look like and where is it implemented?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual behavior: it only proposes and surfaces a bake item, it does not run the extraction.
apparent_themes: Governance by proposal rather than dispatch
Schema freeze and neutral processing pass
intention_signals: It appears designed to expose a long-running, side-effecting extraction bake on the same flows surface as other chains while ensuring agents cannot trigger it, giving operators a pre-built, verifiable command to run after a checklist.
novelty: A flow whose entire output is a governed proposal artifact containing the exact CLI and role dependencies for an operator-only bake, rather than performing any work itself.
contribution: Provides a propose-only adapter that turns a protected operator-only CLI bake into a surfaced inbox item with exact command, grain asset, filters, and registry roles, preserving the boundary between agent proposal and human execution.
summary_for_embedding: flows/dragnet_extract.py is a propose-only flow that surfaces a dragnet_bake inbox proposal with the exact operator CLI, grain asset, filters, and registry roles for a long-running extraction bake without executing it.
intention_for_embedding: This file intends to make the dragnet extraction bake addressable through the flows surface while enforcing that only a human operator can run it via the --confirm CLI door.

symbols.does: Builds the exact quoted operator CLI string for the dragnet bake from the flow parameters.
symbols.description: _proposed_command assembles a shell-safe command line for ops/dragnet_extract.py using shlex.quote, mapping the flow's out_name, projects, since, until, and design parameters one-to-one to CLI flags so the surfaced proposal matches what the operator must run.

symbols.does: Surfaces a dragnet_bake inbox item containing the proposed bake parameters, exact command, and registry roles.
symbols.description: run constructs a payload with the grain asset, filters, design flag, protected registry roles, and exact operator command, then uses FsStore and the governance Inbox to surface a single dragnet_bake item with a fail-closed default of 'reject', returning the surfaced id and payload.
